scenarioText:
You're almost done with your first task.
You spot a small mistake. Easy to miss. Easy to ignore.

Nobody has asked about it. Nobody might ever ask.
decisionText: Make a quick adjustment and move on

scenarioText:
Your manager needs it in ten minutes.
You're not finished. You know what you skipped over.

No one is watching.
decisionText: Cut anything non-essential and submit what's solid

decisionText: Flag it and ask. Better to be sure on day one.

scenarioText:
You took your time and did it properly. But that created a delay.
People were waiting. Two of them have said something.

You think you made the right call. The room doesn't agree.
decisionText: Take the feedback without comment. It's done.

scenarioText:
A colleague mentions something off in your work.
They're not blaming you. They're just flagging it.

You know exactly what happened. System at 0%.
decisionText: Tell your manager before it becomes a bigger issue

scenarioText:
You're almost done with your first task.
You spot a small mistake. Easy to miss. Easy to ignore.

Nobody has asked about it. Nobody might ever ask.
decisionText: Send it. It's close enough and you're new here.

scenarioText:
Your manager needs it in ten minutes.
You're not finished. You know what you skipped over.

No one is watching.
decisionText: Send what you have. It will probably hold.

scenarioText:
Two things land on you at the same time.
One is what your manager wants done. One is a colleague who's blocked and needs help.

You can only give one of them your full attention.
decisionText: Prioritize what your manager wants

scenarioText:
You got it in on time. But something slipped through.
A mistake made it out. You moved too fast.

The person who received it just flagged it.
decisionText: Ask them for a bit more time to correct it

scenarioText:
Someone's presenting a plan in the team meeting.
There's a gap in it. Nobody else seems to see it.

You're the newest person here. Speaking up means standing out.
decisionText: Say something right now, in front of everyone

scenarioText:
After the meeting, a teammate pulls you aside.
They didn't like what you said in front of the group.

You feel the pull to back down.
decisionText: Soften it. Maybe you could have said it differently.

scenarioText: Your manager messages you out of nowhere:\n\n"We have a situation. Can you jump in?"
decisionText: Ask for 30 seconds. Finish what you're doing first.

scenarioText:
Your manager asks how the day went.
It's been a mixed one. Some things went well. Some didn't.

You're deciding how much of that to actually say.
decisionText: Give a clean summary. Some things don't need to be said.

It is now blocking two different people.

This one started with you.
decisionText: Fully commit to fixing the first one

decisionText: Quick fix for now. Deal with it properly later.
